name: Cinnamon Honey Ale
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure and add 3 1/2 pounds of Laaglander Amber dry malt to a brewing vessel.
2. Add 1 1/2 ounces of Cascade hops that will be boiled for one hour.
3. In separate containers, measure out 6 inches of cinnamon bark.
4. Add 2 1/2 pounds of raw honey to the brewing vessel.
5. Boil the mixture for one hour, skimming the surface every few minutes to remove any debris from the unfiltered honey.
6. Lightly crush the cinnamon bark and add it during the final 10 minutes of boiling.
7. Add 1/2 ounce of Cascade hops that will be used as finishing hops, about three minutes before the end of the boil.
8. Sparge the brewing vessel, cool to room temperature, and siphon into a primary fermentation container, topping off to 5 gallons with pre-boiled water.
9. Pitch the yeast and allow it to ferment.
10. After fermentation, prime with 3/4 cup of dry malt boiled in 1 1/2 cups of water.
11. Bottle the beer and let it age for at least one month before drinking. This honey-based brew will improve with age.
